---
title: "Title of your article"
# Authors reference shared affiliations by `id`. Each affiliation needs a
# `name` (organisation) and `country` (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2, e.g. NL, US, DE);
# `department` is optional. An author can list multiple affiliations.
author:
  - name: First Author
    affiliations:
      - ref: vu
  - name: Second Author
    affiliations:
      - ref: vu
      - ref: uva
  - name: Third Author
    affiliations:
      - ref: uva
affiliations:
  - id: vu
    department: Department of Communication Science
    name: Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam
    country: NL
  - id: uva
    department: Amsterdam School of Communication Research
    name: University of Amsterdam
    country: NL
abstract: |
  A brief abstract that describes the study and main findings.
  In this template document, we will mainly give some examples of using Quarto.
keywords: some, useful, keywords
volume: "?"
pubnumber: "?"
pubyear: "20??"
firstpage: 1
bibliography: bibliography.bib
doi: "10.5117/CCR????"
shortauthors: First et al.
format:
  ccr-pdf:
    keep-tex: true
# uncomment to get version for submission (anonymous and with watermark)
# classoption:
#   - review
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>. 

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can also use nice mathematics: 

$$
  \frac{1}{2\pi i}\int_\gamma\! f = \sum_{k=1}^m \\
  n(\gamma;a_k) \text{Res}(f;a_k)\,.
$$

To learn more about this journal, read @atteveldt:2019:RCC.

## References
